content.full_text:
August 9, 1994

Burk E. Delventhal
Office of City Attorney
City and County of San Francisco
Room 306 City Hall
San Francisco, CA 94102-4682

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-207

Dear Mr. Delventhal:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of the Mayor of the City and County of San Francisco
regarding the "gift" provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹

QUESTION

If the Mayor of the City and County of San Francisco accepts
a gift from PG & E of a vehicle conversion kit for exclusive use
in the vehicle assigned to the Office of the Mayor would a gift to
the mayor result?

CONCLUSION

If PG & E limits the use of the gift to the vehicle assigned
to the Office of the Mayor, a gift to the Mayor would result.

FACTS

The City and County of San Francisco (the "City"), in
coordination with the Air Resources Board, is in the process of
evaluating technologies for reducing emission levels in city
vehicles. The primary purpose for the project is to better
evaluate future purchases of city vehicles to comply with the
State's future mandated lower emission standards.

___________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000 - 18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-94-207
Page 2


     As part of the project, Pacific, Gas and Electric (PG & E)
proposes to make a gift of the use of a clean air vehicle
conversion kit, and its installation, to the City and County of
San Francisco for a period of approximately two years. PG & E
will utilize the data from the project for its own evaluation of
the equipment.

     PG & E wishes to limit the use of the conversion kit to the
city vehicle assigned to the Office of the Mayor, for use during
official city business. The Office of the Mayor currently has one
vehicle assigned to it which is used by the person who holds the
Office of the Mayor for official city business. The approximate
value of the kit and conversion is $2,500 to $3,000.

     San Francisco Administrative Code Section 10.116 authorizes
department heads, including the Mayor, to accept gifts directly on
behalf of the City which do not exceed $5,000, where the gifts are
made for the use of the city in connection with a matter under the
control of the department head.

                              ANALYSIS

     Section 82028 defines the term "gift" and provides in
pertinent part:

          (a)  "Gift" means, except as provided in
     subdivision (b), any payment to the extent that
     consideration of equal or greater value is not
     received....

     The use of the conversion kit and its installation and
removal constitutes a gift² under the Act.  To determine whether a
gift³ has been made to an official's agency, and not to the
official who uses it, Regulation 18944.2(a) provides that the
following criteria must be satisfied:

          (1)  The agency receives and controls the
     payment.


___________

2  A "payment" means a payment, distribution, transfer, loan
advance, deposit, a gift or other rendering of money, property,
services or anything else of value, whether tangible or
intangible. (Section 82044.)

3  We have advised that, under some circumstances, a donation of
tangible property constitutes a gift to a governmental entity to
which an official belongs and not to the public official. (See
for example, Raye Advice Letter, A-84-077; Leidigh Advice Letter,
No. L-94-129.) In making this determination, we have considered a
number of factors, including whether the donor relinquished total
control of the gift to the government. It does not appear that
the same rationale is applicable to your facts.

Our File No. A-94-207
Page 3


     (2)  The payment is used for official agency
business.

     (3)  The agency, in its sole discretion,
determines the specific official or officials who
shall use the payment.  However, the donor may
identify a specific purpose for the agency's use of
the payment, so long as the donor does not
designate the specific official or officials who
may use the payment.

     (4)  The agency memorializes the payment in a
written public record which embodies the
requirements of subdivisions (a)(1) to (a)(3) of
this regulation set forth above and which:

          (A)  Identifies the donor and the
officials, officials, or class of officials
receiving or using the payment;

          (B)  Describes the official agency use
and the nature and amount of the payment; and

          (C)  Is filed with the agency official
who maintains the records of the agency's
statements of economic interests where the
agency has a specific office for the
maintenance of such statements, or where no
specific office exists for the maintenance of
agency such statements, at a designated office of the
agency, and the filing is done within 30 days
of the receipt of the payment by the agency.

     You have indicated that PG & E proposes to make a gift of a
clean air vehicle conversion kit, and its installation, to the
City and County of San Francisco for a period of approximately two
years.  Of course, the City and County of San Francisco is a
permissible agency which may receive and control the payment.
(Regulation 18944.1(a)(1); Sections 82003 and 82041.)

     However, PG & E wishes to limit the use of the conversion kit
to the city vehicle assigned to the Office of the Mayor.  The
vehicle is used by the Mayor.  Thus, the question you pose is
whether PG & E can make a gift to the city, but limit its use for
a specific official, without the official having received a gift.

     Regulation 18944.2 sets forth specific criteria for
determining when a gift under the Act is a gift to an agency
rather than to the public official who benefits from or uses the
gift.  Under the regulation, if a public official who is the
intended recipient of a gift accepts the gift on behalf of his or
her agency, the official has received a reportable gift.

Our File No. A-94-207
Page 4


     Specifically, pursuant to subdivision (a)(3) of Regulation
18944.2, the agency in its sole discretion must determine the
specific official or officials who shall use the payment. In
addition, while the donor may identify a specific purpose⁴ for the
agency's use of the payment, the donor cannot designate the
specific official or official who shall use the gift.

     As noted above, PG & E wishes to limit the use of the
conversion kit to the city vehicle assigned to the Office of the
Mayor, which is for the Mayor's use or a person acting as the
Mayor. We conclude that if PG & E limits the "use" of the
equipment to the Office of the Mayor as you have described, the
donor has designated a specific official for purposes of the
regulation, and, therefore, the agency will not, in its own
discretion, determine the specific official or officials who shall
use the gift. Consequently, a gift to the Mayor would result.

     Accordingly, it would be contrary to the provisions of the
regulation for the Mayor to accept the gift on behalf of the City
and County of San Francisco under these facts.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.⁵

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Luisa Menchaca
                                            Counsel, Legal Division




_________________
     ⁴ This language was included in the regulation to clarify that
while a donor may not determine who shall use a gift, a donor may
state a governmental purpose for the agency's use of the payment.
(Staff Memorandum to the Commission regarding the proposed
adoption of Regulation 18944.2, page 4, March 28, 1994.) It
appears PG & E wishes to assist the City in its evaluation of
technologies to reduce air emissions by loaning its equipment to
the City for approximately two years. Therefore, a specific
purpose for the making of the gift has been identified.

     ⁵ Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
